steps.text: Develop a written emergency action plan, keep it in the workplace, and make it available to employees for review.
Designate and train employees to assist in a safe and orderly evacuation of other employees.
Review the emergency action plan with each employee covered by the plan.
Develop a written fire prevention plan, keep it in the workplace, and make it available to employees for review.
Inform employees upon initial assignment to a job of the fire hazards to which they are exposed.
Review with each employee those parts of the fire prevention plan necessary for self-protection.
Train all employees who operate working platforms in safety hazards, emergency action plan procedures, work procedures, and personal fall arrest system inspection, care, and use.
Certify that employees have been trained in operating and inspecting a working platform by preparing a certification record.
Completely deflate tires before demounting by removal of the valve core.
Apply rubber lubricant to bead and rim mating surfaces during assembly of the wheel and inflation of the tire.
Inspect the tire and wheel components after tire inflation while still within the restraining device.

preconditions.text: When the plan is developed or the employee is assigned initially to a job.
When the employee's responsibilities under the plan change.
Before using a personal fall arrest system, and after any component or system is changed.

constraints.text: An employer with 10 or fewer employees may communicate the plan orally to employees.
Working platforms shall not be loaded in excess of the rated load, as stated on the platform load rating plate.
Employees shall be prohibited from working on snow, ice, or other slippery material covering platforms, except for the removal of such materials.
The platform shall not be operated in winds in excess of 25 miles per hour (40.2 km/hr) except to move it from an operating to a storage position.
Employees on working platforms shall be protected by a personal fall arrest system.
No attempt shall be made to correct the seating of side and lock rings by hammering, striking or forcing the components while the tire is pressurized.
Cracked, broken, bent or otherwise damaged rim components shall not be reworked, welded, brazed, or otherwise heated.
Employees shall stay out of the trajectory when inflating a tire.
